description: Make auth.json the single source of truth for the WebSocket secret, remove secret from config.json, decouple port configuration to the extension side panel, and add specific error states for auth vs connection failures.

userStories.title: Remove secret from OpentabsConfig and config.json
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want the secret removed from config.json so that config corruption (tests, manual editing) can never break the extension's WebSocket connection.
acceptanceCriteria: OpentabsConfig interface no longer has a 'secret' field
parseConfigRecord no longer parses or returns 'secret'
saveConfig and saveToolConfig no longer read/write 'secret' to config.json
loadConfig no longer generates or recovers secret — it returns config without secret
Existing config.json files with a 'secret' field are silently ignored (no migration error)
userStories.notes:
Files to change:
- platform/mcp-server/src/config.ts: Remove 'secret' from OpentabsConfig (line 52-53), parseConfigRecord (lines 245, 316, 318), loadConfig secret generation block (lines 360-380), saveToolConfig secret preservation (line 441), first-run default config secret generation (line 344). Keep readSecretFromAuthFile and writeAuthFile — they're needed by US-002. Keep generateSecret — it's needed by US-002.
- platform/mcp-server/src/config.test.ts: Update all tests that set/check 'secret' in config (lines 51-52, 71, 78, 87, 101, 109-120, 129, 151, 171, 188, 202, 237, 264). Remove the 'generates secret if missing' test. Tests for writeAuthFile and readSecretFromAuthFile should remain.
- platform/cli/src/config.test.ts: Update test at line 45-47 that includes 'secret' in config.

userStories.title: Server reads/writes secret exclusively from auth.json
userStories.description: As a user, I want the server to read the secret from auth.json on startup so that the secret never changes unless explicitly rotated, and the extension's cached auth.json always matches.
acceptanceCriteria: Server startup reads secret from ~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json via readSecretFromAuthFile
If auth.json exists with a valid secret, server uses it (no new secret generated, no write)
If auth.json does not exist, server generates a new secret and writes auth.json
auth.json format is { "secret": "<hex>" } with no port field
state.wsSecret is set from auth.json, not from config
Server never overwrites an existing auth.json on startup
userStories.notes:
Files to change:
- platform/mcp-server/src/config.ts: Update writeAuthFile to only write { secret } (remove port parameter and port field). Export a new loadSecret() function that reads from auth.json or generates+writes a new one.
- platform/mcp-server/src/index.ts: On startup (around line 221), call loadSecret() to get the secret and set state.wsSecret. Remove the existing writeAuthFile call — the write only happens inside loadSecret when auth.json doesn't exist.
- platform/mcp-server/src/reload.ts: Line 180 sets state.wsSecret from config.secret — remove this line. Secret is immutable after startup, only changes on explicit rotation.
- platform/mcp-server/src/config.test.ts: Update writeAuthFile tests (lines 287-312) to expect { secret } without port.
- platform/mcp-server/src/reload.test.ts: Line 117 expects state.wsSecret from config — update to not change on reload.

userStories.title: CLI commands read secret from auth.json instead of config.json
userStories.description: As a user, I want CLI commands (status, audit, plugin, reload) to read the secret from auth.json so they work correctly after the secret is removed from config.json.
acceptanceCriteria: opentabs config show no longer displays a 'secret' field
opentabs config rotate-secret writes only to auth.json (not config.json)
opentabs config rotate-secret warns user to reload extension from chrome://extensions/ if extension is not connected
All CLI commands gracefully handle missing auth.json (proceed without auth)
userStories.notes:
Files to change:
- platform/cli/src/commands/status.ts: Line 58 reads config.secret — change to read from auth.json. Create a shared helper readAuthSecret() in platform/cli/src/config.ts that reads ~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json.
- platform/cli/src/commands/audit.ts: Line 188 reads config.secret — use readAuthSecret().
- platform/cli/src/commands/plugin.ts: Line 52 reads config.secret — use readAuthSecret().
- platform/cli/src/commands/config.ts: Lines 14-16 redactSecret — remove entirely (no secret in config to redact). Lines 382-431 handleRotateSecret — generate new secret, write auth.json only, notify running server with old secret to tell extension to reload, warn user to manually reload extension if not connected.

userStories.title: opentabs-plugin build reads secret from auth.json
userStories.description: As a plugin developer, I want opentabs-plugin build to read the secret from auth.json so that POST /reload works after the secret is removed from config.json.
acceptanceCriteria: notifyServer in build.ts reads secret from ~/.opentabs/extension/auth.json instead of config.json
If auth.json doesn't exist or has no secret, notifyServer silently skips (no error)
userStories.notes:
Files to change:
- platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts: Lines 100-129 notifyServer function. Currently reads config.json and extracts record.secret (line 110). Change to read auth.json. Construct the path as join(getConfigDir(), 'extension', 'auth.json'). Parse JSON, extract 'secret' field.

userStories.title: Offscreen document reads port from chrome.storage.local instead of auth.json
userStories.description: As a user, I want the extension to use a configurable port from chrome.storage.local so that port changes take effect without an extension reload.
acceptanceCriteria: Offscreen document reads port from chrome.storage.local on startup (default 9515)
auth.json is only read for the secret, not the port
bootstrapFromAuthFile no longer extracts port from auth.json
refreshWsUrl uses the stored port for /ws-info requests
connect() uses the stored port for ws:// URL
userStories.notes:
Files to change:
- platform/browser-extension/src/offscreen/index.ts: bootstrapFromAuthFile (around line 470) currently reads { secret, port } from auth.json — change to only read secret. Add a function to read port from chrome.storage.local with default 9515. refreshWsUrl (around line 185) constructs the /ws-info URL — use the stored port. connect() (around line 261) constructs the ws:// URL — use the stored port.

userStories.title: Offscreen document listens for port changes and reconnects
userStories.description: As a user, I want the extension to reconnect when I change the port in the side panel without needing to reload the extension.
acceptanceCriteria: Offscreen document listens for a 'port-changed' message from the background script
On port change, offscreen document closes current WebSocket and reconnects to the new port
Background script relays port change messages from the side panel to the offscreen document
userStories.notes:
Files to change:
- platform/browser-extension/src/offscreen/index.ts: Add a chrome.runtime.onMessage listener for { type: 'port-changed', port: number }. On receipt, update the stored port, close the current WebSocket, and call scheduleReconnect(0) to reconnect immediately.
- platform/browser-extension/src/background.ts: Add a relay handler — when the side panel sends a port-changed message, forward it to the offscreen document via chrome.runtime.sendMessage.

userStories.title: Side panel footer: port display and edit on right side
userStories.description: As a user, I want to see and change the MCP server port in the side panel footer so I know which port the extension is trying to connect to.
acceptanceCriteria: Footer shows the current port on the right side (existing GitHub + theme toggle stay on the left)
Port is displayed as a subtle label like 'Port: 9515' using existing retro UI text styles
Clicking the port text turns it into an inline editable Input using the existing retro Input component
Pressing Enter or blurring the input saves the new port to chrome.storage.local and sends a port-changed message
Invalid port values (non-numeric, out of 1-65535 range) are rejected with the Input border turning destructive
UI matches the existing neo-brutalist retro design system (border-2, shadow-md, font-head/font-mono, no border-radius)
userStories.notes:
Files to change:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/Footer.tsx: Currently has a left-aligned flex row with GitHub link and theme toggle buttons. Add a right-aligned section (use ml-auto or justify-between on the flex container) showing the port. Use the existing retro components: Text for the label, Input for the edit field, Button if needed.
- Use existing retro UI components from platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/retro/ — specifically Input.tsx, Text.tsx, Button.tsx. If any new retro UI primitives are needed, reference ~/Downloads/RetroUI-main/components/ for the design patterns.
- Store/read port from chrome.storage.local under key 'serverPort'. On save, send { type: 'port-changed', port } via chrome.runtime.sendMessage.
- The footer layout should be: [GitHub icon] [Theme toggle] ... [Port: 9515] with the port pushed to the right.

userStories.title: Side panel: distinct error states for connection refused vs auth failure
userStories.description: As a user, I want the side panel to show different error messages for 'server not running' vs 'secret mismatch' so I know exactly what to do to fix the issue.
acceptanceCriteria: When the server is unreachable (connection refused / network error), the side panel shows 'Cannot Reach MCP Server' with 'opentabs start --port <N>' where N is the configured port
When the server responds with 401 (secret mismatch), the side panel shows 'Authentication Failed' with instructions to reload the extension from chrome://extensions/
The offscreen document communicates the disconnect reason (connection_refused vs auth_failed) to the side panel via chrome.runtime.sendMessage or storage
Both error states use the existing Empty component with destructive border/separator styling
userStories.notes:
Files to change:
- platform/browser-extension/src/offscreen/index.ts: When /ws-info returns 401, send a message or set storage indicating 'auth_failed'. When connection fails entirely, indicate 'connection_refused'. Currently the offscreen doc sends connection state via chrome.runtime.sendMessage — extend the protocol to include a disconnect reason.
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/EmptyStates.tsx: The DisconnectedState currently shows a single message. Split into two states based on the disconnect reason. Use the existing Empty compound component with destructive styling. For auth failure, show 'Authentication Failed' title with instruction to reload extension. For connection refused, show 'Cannot Reach MCP Server' with 'opentabs start --port <N>'.
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/App.tsx: Pass the disconnect reason to the rendered state component.

userStories.title: Update E2E test fixtures for auth.json-only secret
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want E2E tests to work with the new auth.json-only secret model so that the test suite passes.
acceptanceCriteria: E2E fixtures create auth.json with { "secret": "..." } (no port) in isolated extension dirs
E2E fixtures no longer set secret in config.json
All E2E tests pass with the new secret model
userStories.notes:
Files to change:
- e2e/fixtures.ts: Multiple references to secret in config (lines 195, 227, 372, 476, 745, etc.). These need to write auth.json instead of putting secret in config.json. The fixture creates isolated config dirs — ensure auth.json is written to the correct extension subdirectory within each isolated dir. Check how OPENTABS_CONFIG_DIR affects getExtensionDir() — the extension dir is typically join(configDir, 'extension').

userStories.title: Update CLAUDE.md to document new auth and port architecture
userStories.description: As a platform developer, I want CLAUDE.md to reflect the new secret and port architecture so that future development follows the correct patterns.
acceptanceCriteria: CLAUDE.md documents that auth.json is the single source of truth for the WebSocket secret
CLAUDE.md documents that config.json does not store the secret
CLAUDE.md documents the extension port configuration in the side panel footer
CLAUDE.md documents the secret rotation flow (opentabs config rotate-secret)
CLAUDE.md documents the two distinct error states (connection refused vs auth failure)
No references to 'secret in config.json' remain in CLAUDE.md
userStories.notes:
Update the relevant sections in CLAUDE.md:
- 'MCP Server' section: mention auth.json as secret source, remove references to config.json secret
- 'Chrome Extension' section: mention configurable port in side panel footer stored in chrome.storage.local
- 'Side panel empty states' section: update to describe the two disconnect states (connection refused vs auth failure)
- 'Key Concepts' section: add explanation of auth model and port configuration
- Remove any references to config.json storing the secret
